name: planar_slider_crank_handwritten
_note: A 4th brand-new mechanism authored entirely by hand as data, sharing no geometry/constants with SR6, Stewart or the four-bar. It is the first machine to use a lower pair the four primitives could not express before: the PRISMATIC / slider joint (uam.assembly.PointOnLine). Ground crank-pivot at origin, crank r=30, connecting rod L=90; the rod's far wrist-pin must slide on the ground's x-axis guide. Two revolute joints (Coincident + Parallel about z, keeping the loop in z=0) + one slider (rod.b on the x line). 1 real DOF (crank angle); seeds are a deliberately OPEN pose so the solver must actually close the loop, not sit on the answer.
